title: OpenClaw
oneLiner: Open-source agent platform for browser, tool, and workflow automation that actually takes actions.
summary: OpenClaw is an open-source agent platform for running action-oriented AI workflows across browser automation, tools, skills, local execution, and connected services.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw matters because it represents the shift from chat assistants to agents that operate software. It gives builders a concrete runtime to study browser control, tool execution, skills, safety boundaries, and workflow automation in one place.
bestFor: Builders experimenting with browser and tool-using agents
Teams that want a self-hosted runtime for repeatable automation workflows
Researchers studying agent safety, permissions, and real-world action boundaries
notFor: Users who only want a passive chatbot
Teams unwilling to review sandboxing, credentials, and safety controls before giving an agent tool access
seoTitle: OpenClaw: Agents resource for open AI builders
seoDescription: OpenClaw profile on OpenAgent.bot: what it is, when to use it, official links, open-source status, and structured resource data.
shareDescription: Open-source agent platform for browser, tool, and workflow automation that actually takes actions.

coreStrengths.title: Action-oriented runtime
coreStrengths.description: OpenClaw is designed for agents that can use tools and automate tasks, not just answer questions.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: That makes it a useful reference point for the whole action-agent category.

coreStrengths.title: Browser and workflow surface
coreStrengths.description: Its ecosystem emphasizes browser control, tools, channels, and reusable skills.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Real agent work often happens across websites, APIs, files, and messaging systems.

coreStrengths.title: Open-source inspection path
coreStrengths.description: The public repository lets builders inspect how the runtime handles execution and boundaries.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Action agents require trust, and trust starts with inspectable implementation details.

useCaseNotes.title: Browser-based automation
useCaseNotes.description: Use OpenClaw as a candidate when an agent must navigate websites, fill forms, or inspect pages.

useCaseNotes.title: Reusable agent workflows
useCaseNotes.description: Turn repeated tasks into skills, scripts, or controlled workflows that agents can run again.

useCaseNotes.title: Agent safety research
useCaseNotes.description: Study the risks that appear when agents can execute code, access accounts, and use authenticated browser sessions.

compareNotes.title: OpenClaw is for doing, not just chatting
compareNotes.summary: A chatbot interface is useful for conversation; OpenClaw is relevant when the agent needs tool access and workflow execution.

commandLine.label: Clone the OpenClaw repository
commandLine.description: Start from the official repository and docs before installing daemons, browser access, or connected services.

faq.question: Is OpenClaw open source?
faq.answer: OpenClaw is listed with MIT based on its official source links. Always re-check the repository or model card before production use.
